title: 10 Herbal Creams For Inflamed Mouth
intro_desc: If your mouth is inflamed, you might want to try using herbal creams. These creams are made from plants that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain. One example is Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger. Ginger is great at reducing inflammation, and when applied as a cream, it can bring relief to an inflamed mouth.  You can also try herbal teas to help with an inflamed mouth. Drinking tea made from certain herbs can help reduce inflammation from the inside out. For example, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an inflamed mouth. Drinking licorice root tea can help reduce swelling and ease pain.  Another example is Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory. Drinking turmeric tea can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth. By using herbal teas and creams, you can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in your mouth, making it easier to eat and talk.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth. Gingerol, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to inflammation. Shogaol, on the other hand, has been found to have analgesic properties, helping to numb the affected area and reduce discomfort. By applying Zingiber officinale creams topically, the active constituents can directly target the inflamed area, providing relief and promoting healing.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of boiling water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in the turmeric powder. Let it cool until it's slightly thickened.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, use the Zingiber officinale cream to soothe an inflamed mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and pain in the mouth. It also contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties are further enhanced by its content of triterpenes, specifically 18α-glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Glycyrrhizin also helps to reduce redness and inflammation by blocking the action of a specific enzyme that breaks down cortisol, a hormone that helps to regulate inflammation. By applying Glycyrrhiza glabra creams to an inflamed mouth, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties work together to provide fast and effective relief.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the glycerin dissolves.
Add 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let it cool down. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh.
Mix the cooled mixture with 1 cup of aloe vera gel and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth cream. Store in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory. Turmerones and ferulic acid, two other key constituents, also contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin blocks the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. This helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth, making it easier to eat and speak. The anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa creams also help to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Melt 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a bowl in the microwave for 10 seconds.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the melted coconut oil.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) to the mixture and stir well.
Add 1 cup of distilled water and stir until the mixture is smooth and well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by reducing inflammation and swelling, making them effective in soothing inflamed mouth tissues. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and LOX, which contribute to mouth inflammation. Caffeic acid derivatives, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the mouth. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to the affected area, you can help to calm the inflammation and promote a healthy environment for your mouth to recover.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the coconut oil by mixing it with the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours. Strain the oil and discard the solids.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a separate saucepan over low heat. Once melted, remove from heat.
Combine the infused coconut oil, melted beeswax and shea butter mixture, and vitamin E oil in a mixing bowl. Stir well until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Echinacea purpurea cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area to soothe inflamed mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamin E as its active constituents. Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth. Aloe-emodin has antibacterial properties that prevent infection and promote healing in inflamed areas. Vitamin E has antioxidant properties that protect the mouth from damage caused by free radicals and promote tissue repair. These properties make Aloe barbadensis creams a useful treatment for inflamed mouths, soothing and healing irritated tissues.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 teaspoons of honey.
In a small saucepan, heat the coconut oil over low heat until melted.
Add the beeswax to the saucepan and stir until melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add the aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and honey. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool until solidified. Use as needed to soothe inflamed mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol, caffeic acid, and tannins. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the mouth. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptol, in particular, can help decrease redness and pain associated with inflamed mouth tissues. The antibacterial properties of caffeic acid can also help control the growth of bacteria that may be contributing to the inflammation. By applying eucalyptus globulus creams to the affected area, these compounds can work together to provide relief from mouth inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil, and a few drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals until smooth.
Add beeswax and stir until melted and well combined.
Add Eucalyptus globulus essential oil and vitamin E oil, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Use the cream as needed to soothe inflamed mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the mouth from further damage caused by free radicals. The triterpenoid saponins have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing in the mouth. By combining these properties, Calendula officinalis creams can help to soothe and calm inflamed mouths, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and a small bowl.
Steep the dried Calendula flowers in the coconut oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bowl every day. This will make the oil infused with calendula.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each interval.
Combine the melted beeswax, infused oil, and olive oil in a bowl. Stir well until the mixture is smooth and well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Your Calendula creams for inflamed mouth are now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols, including silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in an inflamed mouth. Silymarin also has antioxidant properties, which can neutralize free radicals and protect the mouth from damage caused by inflammation. The flavonoids in silymarin can also help reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes. By reducing inflammation and protecting the mouth from damage, silymarin in Silybum marianum creams can help alleviate the symptoms of an inflamed mouth.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and a small saucepan.
Combine the dried Silybum marianum flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add the beeswax to the mixture and heat it over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool. Pour it into small containers and let it solidify. Once set, use as a cream for inflamed mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains the active constituents cineole and terpinen-4-ol. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight off infections in the mouth. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to have a strong effect on bacteria and viruses that can cause inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of terpinen-4-ol also help to soothe and calm irritated tissue, reducing pain and discomfort. By applying Melaleuca alternifolia creams to the affected area, you can potentially reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil, and 1 teaspoon of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix the shea butter and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Slowly add the shea butter mixture to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture, stirring constantly.
Add the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, tannins, and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues in the mouth. The rosmarinic acid in Melissa officinalis creams has a potent ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to inflammation and pain. The tannins in the cream have astringent properties that help to reduce bleeding and ease inflammation, while the flavonoids have antioxidant properties that protect the mouth tissues from damage caused by free radicals. By applying Melissa officinalis creams to an inflamed mouth, the combination of these active constituents can help to calm and comfort the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for inflamed mouth
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (optional).
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until the wax is melted.
Add the Melissa officinalis flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Add the vitamin E oil and essential oil (if using) to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers or tubes and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in a cool, dark place and use as needed to soothe inflamed mouth.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe an inflamed mouth. Certain herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritated tissues. They can also help reduce swelling and ease pain. When you drink herbal tea, the active ingredients can reach the affected area and start to work their soothing magic.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good choice for soothing an inflamed mouth, but be careful not to overdo it. Drinking them every day can cause some herbal teas to lose their effectiveness. Also, some herbs can interact with medications or worsen certain health conditions, so it's essential to be mindful of what you're consuming.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help soothe an inflamed mouth, but it takes some time to see results. You might feel a bit better after a few hours, but it can take up to 24 to 48 hours for the inflammation to really start to calm down. The exact time depends on the severity of the inflammation and the type of herbal tea you're using.
faq_time: When you have an inflamed mouth, try drinking herbal tea in the morning. This way, its soothing effects can help calm the inflammation throughout the day. The caffeine in tea can also help reduce pain and discomfort, making it easier to eat and speak.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for an inflamed mouth. Some herbal teas, like ginkgo biloba and garlic, may slow blood clotting and increase the risk of bleeding. Other teas, like feverfew, might also affect blood thinners or pain medications. This can cause problems or make conditions worse.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and have an inflamed mouth, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas might be safe, but others can be too strong. For example, sage and peppermint teas might calm your mouth, but it's not clear how they'll affect your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for an inflamed mouth, but it's best to check the ingredients first. Some herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula are known to be safe for breastfeeding mothers. However, be cautious of teas containing sage, eucalyptus, and ginger, as they can affect milk supply or interact with medications.
